paper_title: Juniperus communis Pollen Viability and Germination Study

methods.description: Collection and storage of Juniperus communis pollen for viability and germination studies.

steps.description: Twigs with mature microstrobili were cut separately from 5 shrubs and transferred to vessels with water and taken to the laboratory 2 days before discharging the pollen from microsporangia.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and transport juniper twigs with mature microstrobili to the lab

steps.description: Released pollen grains were collected on a large sheet beneath the vessels and sieved.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and sieve released pollen grains

steps.description: The pollen grains of each individual were, after sieving, left in the laboratory for one additional day to dry more completely and used immediately in a germination test.
steps.searchable_summary: dry pollen grains for germination test

steps.description: The remaining dried pollen was divided into two parts in each tested shrub. One part of the pollen was stored at + 4 °C, the other at − 20 °C.

methods.description: Procedure for testing pollen germination and viability under controlled conditions.

steps.description: Pollen germination was carried out at 25 °C in the dark with a separate evaluation of pollen viability in each shrub under study.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct pollen germination test at 25°C in the dark

steps.description: The cultivation media consisted of 1.5% agar and 5% sucrose.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare cultivation media with agar and sucrose

steps.description: The pollen was evenly dusted over the gel surface in small Petri dishes by blowing it from a soft-hair brush.
steps.searchable_summary: apply pollen to gel surface in Petri dishes

steps.description: Each dish was placed in a larger Petri dish containing 2 ml H2O and covered with a lid.
steps.searchable_summary: place small Petri dish in larger dish with water

steps.description: The number of germinated pollen grains was recorded from a random sample of 100 pollen grains, and the pollen tube length was measured on a sample of 30 pollen grains on each Petri dish.
steps.searchable_summary: record germination and measure pollen tube length

steps.description: Differences in both viability traits were tested regarding the storage temperature and shrubs using two-way analysis of variance (ANOVA).

methods.description: Staining procedure to visualize mitotic division in pollen grains.

steps.description: Germinating pollen grains were washed out from the cultivation medium surface with 1 N HCl into a thin-walled glass test tube using a pipette.
steps.searchable_summary: wash pollen grains with HCl into test tube

steps.description: The test tube containing pollen was plugged with a rubber stopper and incubated in a water bath (+ 60 °C) for 5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate pollen in water bath at 60°C

steps.description: Feulgen solution was added to the pollen grains stuck on the tube wall.
The test tube was plugged with a stopper and left at room temperature in the dark for 1 h.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate stained pollen at room temperature

steps.description: A few drops of the mixture containing pollen grains were placed on a microscopic glass, covered with a cover glass, and examined under a microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and examine stained pollen under microscope
